GC-CLOUD WAVE 4 — MONTHLY STATUS REPORT
Reporting Period: February 2026
Report Date: March 1, 2026
Project Manager: Carol Nguyen
Status: GREEN

---

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

GC-Cloud Wave 4 is progressing excellently. The team has made strong
progress across all workstreams and stakeholder engagement remains high.
We are confident in our ability to deliver the project on schedule and
within the approved parameters.

---

SCHEDULE HIGHLIGHTS

The project timeline is performing well. While we have experienced some
minor refinements to our internal planning milestones, the project team
has worked proactively to absorb these adjustments through schedule
optimization. Our Q3 2026 delivery commitment remains intact.

Note: The infrastructure assessment phase required a slight adjustment
of approximately three weeks from the original internal planning estimate,
but this has been fully accommodated within the project's existing
schedule contingency. No impact to the final delivery date is anticipated.

---

FINANCIAL OVERVIEW

The project continues to track responsibly against financial parameters.
Our team completed a comprehensive infrastructure assessment in February
which provided valuable insights into the migration architecture. The
findings from this assessment are being used to refine our cost model
to ensure we deliver maximum value for the approved investment.

We are in the process of finalizing updated cost projections which we
expect to share with the finance review board in the coming weeks. Initial
indications suggest the revised figures will be well-supported by the
business case and represent sound stewardship of public funds.

Note (Appendix A): The infrastructure assessment identified additional
middleware remediation requirements not captured in the original scope
baseline. The estimated cost of this remediation is $340,000, representing
approximately 14% above the approved envelope of $2,400,000. A formal
budget revision request will be submitted to the Director for consideration.
The project team does not anticipate this will materially affect project
viability given the strong business case fundamentals.

---

RISKS AND ISSUES

Risk management activities continue as planned. The team maintains a
comprehensive risk register and risk owners are engaged and active.
No material risk escalations are required at this time.

The project environment remains stable and the team is well-positioned
to manage any emerging challenges as the migration phase approaches.

---

STAKEHOLDER ENGAGEMENT

Stakeholder sentiment is positive. The Director's office has expressed
confidence in the project team's ability to deliver. Client department
representatives remain engaged and are participating actively in the
working groups.

---

OUTLOOK

The project team looks forward to entering the implementation phase in
Q2 2026 following completion of the procurement activities. We are
optimistic about our ability to deliver a successful migration that
meets the Government of Canada's cloud-first objectives.

Report prepared by: Carol Nguyen, PMP
Reviewed by: Portfolio Manager
